---
title: "Figure 5 — correlation-distortion collapse on macro_NR: temporal over-counting governed by Delta/tau"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
macro_NR treats consecutive samples as independent, so it over-counts information by exactly the amount that
consecutive samples are correlated. That correlation is set by the sampling interval in units of the channel
relaxation time, Delta/tau = interval_in_tau, and by NOTHING else. Result: the correlation distortion (up to
~200x) collapses onto a single descending curve per parameter as a function of interval_in_tau, INDEPENDENT of
N_ch and of instrument noise (verified: R^2 ~ 0.64-0.80 vs log(interval); R^2 ~ 0 vs N). Fine sampling (small
Delta/tau) -> highly correlated samples -> massive over-counting; coarse sampling (Delta/tau -> large) ->
independent samples -> distortion -> 1. This is the clean, large, mechanism-specific collapse (contrast the
IR SAMPLE distortion, which is the emission-variance balance and is ~1). Numeric run 433ed13,
Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion diagonal, all N_ch, three noises.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
ALGO     <- "NR"
CORR     <- "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion"
NOISES   <- c("0.1", "1", "10")
PIDX     <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB<- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
              unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

read_noise <- function(z) map_dfr(Sys.glob(sprintf(
  "../data/433ed13/figure_3_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_%s_noise_%s_battery_pool.csv", ALGO, z)),
  ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)) %>% mutate(noise = z)
d <- map_dfr(NOISES, read_noise) %>%
  filter(component_path == CORR, statistic == "value", probit == "mean",
         param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)]) %>%
  group_by(param_name, noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau) %>%
  summarise(D = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(D), D > 0) %>%
  mutate(nch_f  = factor(Num_ch),
         noisef = factor(paste("noise", noise), levels = paste("noise", NOISES)),
         param  = factor(param_name, levels = names(PIDX), labels = unname(PARAM_LAB)))
```

```{r r2}
r2 <- function(v, y) {
  b  <- cut(v, 15); w <- tibble(b, y) %>% group_by(b) %>%
    summarise(vv = var(y), n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>% filter(n > 2)
  1 - weighted.mean(w$vv, w$n, na.rm = TRUE) / var(y)
}
d %>% group_by(param_name) %>%
  summarise(R2_vs_Delta_over_tau = round(r2(log10(interval_in_tau), log10(D)), 3),
            R2_vs_Nch            = round(r2(log10(Num_ch), log10(D)), 3),
            Dmax = round(max(D), 0), .groups = "drop") %>% as.data.frame() %>% print()
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.5, fig.height = 7.0}
g <- ggplot(d, aes(interval_in_tau, D, colour = nch_f, shape = noisef,
                   group = interaction(nch_f, noise))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey45", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = .3, alpha = .5) + geom_point(size = 1.1) +
  facet_wrap(~ param, nrow = 2, labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(.01, .1, 1)) + scale_y_log10() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = expression(N[ch]), end = .92, direction = -1) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15), name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]~"  (sampling interval in relaxation times)"),
       y = "correlation distortion (macro_NR, diagonal)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.title = element_text(size = 8.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_NR_correlation_collapse.pdf", g, width = 7.5, height = 7.0)
```
